events.title: Putin, in call with Trump, put forward ideas on Iran, Kremlin says
events.description: Vladimir Putin and ​Donald Trump held a phone call on Wednesday ​in which the Russian leader put forward ideas on resolving the conflict over Iran's nuclear programme, a Kremlin aide ​said. Putin ‌also proposed a repeat of ⁠a temporary ceasefire in Ukraine to mark the anniversary of the end ‌of World War Two next month, the aide ⁠said. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert has more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Putin, in call with Trump, put forward ideas on Iran, Kremlin says

events.title: AI in advertising risks fuelling misinformation crisis, UN warns
events.description: With spending on advertising topping $1 trillion a year worldwide, the United Nations on Wednesday highlighted the untapped power of major brands to shape the future of Artificial Intelligence, warning that a failure to act could deepen a global information integrity crisis.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 AI in advertising risks fuelling misinformation crisis, UN warns

events.title: Russia will not display military equipment at Victory Day parade due to Ukraine threat
events.description: Russia ​will hold a scaled-back version of its Victory Day parade marking the defeat of Nazi Germany in World War II, the Kremlin said on Wednesday. For the first time in nearly two decades, the annual parade on Moscow's Red Square will not feature a military hardware display. The Kremlin said it was due to Ukraine's "terrorist activity".
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia will not display military equipment at Victory Day parade due to Ukraine threat

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council
events.description: The recently extended ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon remains under strain as violence continues, with civilian deaths and injuries reported in Israeli strikes over the weekend. Meanwhile, the Security Council is debating maritime security with the UN chief calling on nations restricting ship movement through the Strait of Hormuz to “let the global economy breathe.” Follow in-depth meeting coverage here. Stay with us for live updates.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 April: Everyone’s paying the price for Hormuz closure, Guterres warns Security Council

events.title: London police say stabbing of 2 Jewish men is declared a terrorist incident
events.description: Two Jewish men were stabbed and injured in a London street on Wednesday, in what police called an act of terrorism. Police arrested a 45-year-old man on suspicion of attempted murder in the city's latest antisemitic attack. The Metropolitan Police said the attack in the Golders Green area left two men, aged 34 and 76, hospitalized with knife wounds.
events.ticker_label: 💥 London police say stabbing of 2 Jewish men is declared a terrorist incident

events.title: Chokepoints and conflict: How the Hormuz crisis is exposing global shipping vulnerabilities
events.description: The blockading of ships in the Strait of Hormuz as a result of the conflict between the United States and Iran has demonstrated how ships and seafarers have become “leverage in geopolitical disputes,” according to the head of the UN’s International Maritime Organization (IMO).

events.title: Darfur: Two decades on, a new generation of children faces 'horrific violence'
events.description: Twenty years after the conflict in Darfur first sparked global outrage, children in the region are once again trapped in a catastrophic cycle of violence, hunger, and displacement – but this time, the world is failing to take notice.

events.title: Families sue OpenAI over failure to report Canada mass shooter’s behavior on ChatGPT
events.description: New lawsuits allege employees urged company to notify authorities months before deadly Tumbler Ridge attack Families of seven victims of a mass shooting at a secondary school in British Columbia are suing OpenAI and the company’s CEO for negligence after it failed to alert authorities to the shooter’s troubling conversations with ChatGPT. The lawsuits, filed on Wednesday in a federal court in San Francisco, allege that the violent intentions of the shooter, identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, were well-known to OpenAI. Employees at the company flagged the shooter’s account eight months before the attack and determined that it posed “a credible and specific threat of gun violence against real people”, according to the lawsuit. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Families sue OpenAI over failure to report Canada mass shooter’s behavior on ChatGPT

events.title: ‘Total peace’ or ‘all-out war’? Colombian voters face stark choice as rebel attacks surge
events.description: As the country prepares to elect a new president, a fierce debate is raging on how to end the decades-long armed conflict for good The landmark 2016 peace deal between the Colombian government and the largest insurgent army in Latin America succeeded in some ways: the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) agreed to lay down their weapons, and the violence that had racked the country was substantially reduced. But the deal alone could not end the decades-long armed conflict for good. Subsequent administrations slow-walked the implementation of the settlement, which was rejected by Farc dissidents and other rebel factions. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘Total peace’ or ‘all-out war’? Colombian voters face stark choice as rebel attacks surge

events.title: New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah
events.description: The New IRA reportedly claimed a car bomb attack on a Belfast police station, threatening further strikes amid alleged Iran and Hezbollah connections.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 New IRA bombing fuels fears of global militant network tied to Iran, Hezbollah

events.title: Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors
events.description: Efforts continue to free two trapped passengers in wreckage after long-distance train collides with commuter train outside Jakarta, injuring 81 The death toll from a train collision near the Indonesian capital Jakarta has risen to 14 with another 84 injured, the train operator said on Tuesday, as rescuers worked to extract survivors still trapped in the wreckage. The collision between a commuter train and a long-distance train happened late on Monday in Bekasi, just outside Jakarta. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🚑 Train collision in Indonesia kills 14 as rescuers work to reach survivors

events.title: World leaders condemn ‘unacceptable’ violence after armed attack disrupts WH Correspondents’ Dinner
events.description: Global leaders expressed relief that Trump was unharmed after an armed suspect allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington.

events.title: Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week
events.description: Pirates appear to be taking advantage of international naval strength being diverted to Middle East Three vessels have been hijacked off the coast of Somalia in the past week, raising fears of a resurgence in piracy around the Horn of Africa, and adding to the woes of the global shipping industry. The merchant vessel Sward was taken over on 26 April, a day after a dhow was seized. These followed the 21 April hijacking of Honour 25, a motor tanker carrying 18,000 barrels of oil, according to the Maritime Security Centre Indian Ocean (MSCIO), the tracking service of the EU’s naval force. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Fears of resurgence in Somali piracy after three vessels hijacked in a week

events.title: At least 14 dead in Indonesia train crash
events.description: At least 14 people have been killed in a train collision in western Indonesia, with another 84 injured, according to the country's train operator.

events.title: World News in Brief: Syria human rights update, Cuba post-hurricane support still vital, impunity and violence in Myanmar
events.description: Three mass graves were recently uncovered in northeastern Syria, including one reportedly at the site of a former detention centre run by the Kurdish-backed Syrian Armed Forces (SDF).
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ World News in Brief: Syria human rights update, Cuba post-hurricane support still vital, impunity and violence in Myanmar

events.title: Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns
events.description: Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance – “an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community,” a senior official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF has said.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns

events.title: Afghan man convicted of conspiracy in deadly suicide bombing at Kabul airport during US withdrawal
events.description: An alleged ISIS-K militant was convicted of a terrorism conspiracy charge tied to the 2021 Kabul airport bombing that killed 13 U.S. service members.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Afghan man convicted of conspiracy in deadly suicide bombing at Kabul airport during US withdrawal

events.title: World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day
events.description: The United Nations has condemned two recent drone attacks in Sudan, one of which left seven dead, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Monday during his regular media briefing in New York.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day

events.title: Trump admin warns Iraq over Iran terror proxies as US reportedly blocks cash payments
events.description: The Trump administration reportedly halted dollar shipments to Baghdad to pressure Iraq into disbanding Iran-backed militias that have attacked U.S. personnel.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump admin warns Iraq over Iran terror proxies as US reportedly blocks cash payments

events.title: Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say
events.description: They are among nine people killed in successive attacks on Tuesday, which Israel's military says targeted a Hezbollah commander.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israeli 'double-tap' strike kills three rescue workers in Lebanon, officials say

events.title: UK: London stabbing being treated as 'terrorist incident'
events.description: UK police have declared the stabbing of two men in a traditionally Jewish area of London as a "terrorist incident." The victims are in a stable condition. Prime Minister Starmer called the incident "deeply concerning."
events.ticker_label: 💥 UK: London stabbing being treated as 'terrorist incident'

events.title: Anthony Albanese rules out gas export tax on existing contracts and criticises ‘populist’ campaign
events.description: Prime minister says the middle of a global fuel crisis is ‘the worst possible time to jeopardise’ Australia’s partnerships with Asian trading partners Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Anthony Albanese has confirmed next month’s federal budget will not include a new tax on existing gas export contracts as he criticised the “populist” campaign calling for a levy on producers. As reported last week , the prime minister was poised to reject pressure to introduce a 25% tax on gas exports amid concerns the intervention could alienate the Asian trading partners Australia is relying on for supplies of diesel and petrol. Continue reading...

events.title: Nearly all of Europe had above-average heat last year, as climate records toppled
events.description: Almost all of Europe experienced above-average heat in 2025, a year that broke records for wildfires, sea temperatures ​and heatwaves ‌as climate change worsens, EU scientists and the World Meteorological Organization said ⁠on Wednesday. The findings set out how climate change is having increasingly severe consequences in ​Europe, at a time when some governments seek to weaken emissions-cutting policies ‌over economic concerns.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Nearly all of Europe had above-average heat last year, as climate records toppled

events.title: Ukraine 'clearly has the Kremlin rattled'
events.description: No description available.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine 'clearly has the Kremlin rattled'

events.title: Death toll from Colombia bus bombing rises to 20 during wave of violence
events.description: Officials say 20 people died after an explosive device detonated on a bus in southwestern Colombia's Cauca region, injuring 36 others Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Death toll from Colombia bus bombing rises to 20 during wave of violence

events.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
events.description: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress

events.title: Israel in Lebanon: Is the 'security buffer zone' legal?
events.description: Buffer zones occupy a grey area in international humanitarian law, as rules around them in contemporary warfare are not particularly clear.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Israel in Lebanon: Is the 'security buffer zone' legal?

events.title: Could this conference be a 'turning point' for the world's use of fossil fuels?
events.description: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas and coal.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Could this conference be a 'turning point' for the world's use of fossil fuels?

events.title: From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks
events.description: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to send shockwaves through global food systems, the UN Food and Agriculture Organization has warned, as a widening crisis across the Middle East continues to strain humanitarian operations and deepen instability.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 From Hormuz to Lebanon, crisis reverberates through trade routes, upending humanitarian networks

events.title: Mali: Guterres calls for international solutions to curb spread of violent extremism in the Sahel
events.description: There are reports of continuing clashes in Mali on Sunday, a day after a series of coordinated attacks across the landlocked African nation against Government forces by extremists and northern separatist rebels.

events.title: South Pacific islanders strive to rescue their home from toxic legacy
events.description: More than 80 years after World War Two, the Solomon Islands remain one of the most heavily mine-contaminated places in the Pacific.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 South Pacific islanders strive to rescue their home from toxic legacy

events.title: Red cards can be given to players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents
events.description: The International Football Association Board approved a rule that would penalize players with a red card if they cover their mouths when confronting another player. The measure will be in place at this summer's World Cup.

events.title: Global task force dismantles 'pig-butchering' crypto fraud rings preying on Americans
events.description: The FBI and international police forces arrested 276 suspects in a global operation targeting cryptocurrency pig-butchering scam centers overseas.

events.title: 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism
events.description: Two men were stabbed in north London's Barnet borough, prompting a counterterrorism-led investigation into a potentially antisemitic incident.
events.ticker_label: 💥 2 Jewish men stabbed in London attack classified as terrorism

events.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
events.description: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home

events.title: Football: Afghan women's team recognized in blow to Taliban
events.description: Banned from playing football and sent into exile, Afghanistan's women's team has had to fight to regain international status. That newly awarded recognition is a "hard slap to the face" of the Taliban, a player told DW.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Football: Afghan women's team recognized in blow to Taliban

events.title: Europol task force nets 280 arrests as 'violence for hire' spreads across Europe
events.description: European Union police agency Europol says that an international task force formed to crack down on violence as a criminal service has led to 280 arrests in its first year

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
events.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political affairs official Khaled Khiari warned of increasingly dire conditions in Gaza and the West Bank, while former UK Prime Minister and US-led Board of Peace member, Tony Blair, said the council needs to mobilise funds for recovery at this “pivotal juncture”. You can follow all the UN's meetings coverage here but follow live and in-depth updates below.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns

events.title: Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine
events.description: Russia says the annual commemoration of Soviet victory in World War Two will not feature military vehicles or cadets.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia scales back Moscow Victory Day parade, blaming threat from Ukraine

events.title: Over half of South Sudan’s population faces acute hunger crisis
events.description: Conflict and displacement are intensifying South Sudan’s hunger crisis, with 7.8 million people facing high levels of acute food insecurity while 2.2 million children are suffering from acute malnutrition, according to a joint statement on Tuesday from UN agencies.

events.title: WHO calls for stepped up action to eliminate viral hepatitis
events.description: Countries are making measurable progress in combatting viral hepatitis, but the disease remains a major global health challenge, the World Health Organization (WHO) said in a new report published on Tuesday.

events.title: UK’s ‘terrorism’ laws risk overreach, watchdog warns
events.description: UK watchdog warns against overreach in targeting activist groups under 'counterterrorism' laws.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK’s ‘terrorism’ laws risk overreach, watchdog warns

events.title: Last Kenyan police officers leave Haiti after unsuccessful security mission
events.description: All of the Kenyan police officers deployed to gang-plagued Haiti as part of a security support mission have left the country, a Haitian source confirmed Tuesday, as another UN-approved operation gets up and running. Kenya took the lead in the Multinational Security Support Mission (MSS), greenlit by the UN Security Council in late 2023 to help Haiti's overwhelmed police secure the country from powerful gangs. However, armed groups remain in control of significant territory and continue to inflict devastating violence in the Caribbean nation.

events.title: As war nears two months, displaced Lebanese families sink into despair
events.description: It has been nearly two months, ​but Rabih Khreiss still has trouble recognising his new life. The father-of-nine could once put food on the table through his car workshop in southern Lebanon, but is now barely surviving in a tent in the capital Beirut. Like so many others, the Khreiss family fled their southern hometown of Khiyam in the early ​hours of March ‌2, moments after learning that the Hezbollah armed group had fired into Israel in what would become the opening salvos ⁠of a new war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As war nears two months, displaced Lebanese families sink into despair

events.title: Solar power soars to new heights but fears over 'dangerously high' temperatures
events.description: Almost the entirety of Europe saw above-average temperatures last year, while wildfires burnt a record area and heatwaves struck from north to south, according to an extensive new climate study.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Solar power soars to new heights but fears over 'dangerously high' temperatures

events.title: As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief
events.description: With top diplomats gathered at UN Headquarters to review the 1970 Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), the UN Secretary-General warned that it must evolve to survive the age of AI and other new technologies.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief

events.title: Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report
events.description: Human rights groups allege Russia recruits tens of thousands of vulnerable migrants from over 130 countries to fight in its ongoing war against Ukraine.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russia built global recruitment pipeline targeting vulnerable migrants for Ukraine war: report

events.title: Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'
events.description: Trump canceled planned Iran talks in Pakistan, citing regime infighting, and experts say Tehran's good cop, bad cop negotiating tactic has collapsed.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'

events.title: King Charles’s US visit ‘about future generations, not just the current administration’
events.description: Angela Diffley is pleased to welcome Dr. Garret J. Martin, Hurst Senior Professorial Lecturer, and the Co-Director of the Transatlantic Policy Center, in the School of International Service at American University. The US-UK transatlantic relationship is navigating a period of unprecedented friction. While diplomacy through a state visit from King Charles can help sustain dialogue, it cannot repair the growing transatlantic divide triggered by increasing uncertainty over America's alliance with NATO & Europe and the “special relationship” with the UK.

events.title: 89-year-old man allegedly opens fire with shotgun in Greek capital, injuring 4
events.description: An 89-year-old man allegedly opened fire inside two Athens government buildings, wounding at least four people in a rare shooting incident in Greece.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 89-year-old man allegedly opens fire with shotgun in Greek capital, injuring 4

events.title: Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack
events.description: In the two months since the deadly strike, the US defence department has said only that the incident is under investigation.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Former US officials criticise Pentagon silence on deadly Iran school attack

events.title: Families sue OpenAI, alleging chatbot aided in Canadian school shooting
events.description: Plaintiffs accuse OpenAI of not alerting authorities to signs of a threat, leading to a school shooting in February.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Families sue OpenAI, alleging chatbot aided in Canadian school shooting

events.title: Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid
events.description: Soaring oil prices and the blockade are preventing food, fuel and medicine being delivered to millions of people in desperate need, say NGOs The volatility of global oil prices caused by the US and Israel’s war on Iran is taking a toll on the most vulnerable people, by slowing or blocking food and medical aid from reaching them. Now aid organisations are calling for a “humanitarian corridor” to be opened through the strait of Hormuz amid rocketing transportation costs. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Calls for humanitarian corridor through strait of Hormuz as Iran war hits vital aid

events.title: Guardian Essential poll: Australians want higher tax on gas exports and extension of petrol excise cut
events.description: The fuel crisis is seeing more voters keen to shift to renewable energy rather than stick with fossil fuels Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A majority of Australians support taxing profits from gas exports and extending the cut to the fuel excise, according to the latest Guardian Essential poll, despite Anthony Albanese on Wednesday ruling out a new tax on existing gas export contracts . The poll also found the fuel crisis is seeing more voters keen to shift to renewable energy rather than stick with fossil fuels. Australians also say they’re already cutting back on travel, switching to public transport and reducing their use of aircon and heating amid the global fuel uncertainty. Continue reading...

events.title: Renewables rising, Part 2: Seeking stability amid volatile fossil fuel markets
events.description: As global energy markets remain volatile and the price of oil high due to instability and conflict in the Middle East, more countries are looking at investing in renewables to shield their economies from the shocks caused by fossil fuel reliance, rein in import bills – and meet key climate action targets.

events.title: Court sentences former world leader to 7 years in prison for resisting arrest, other charges
events.description: Ousted South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol sentenced to seven years in prison after martial law decree that triggered a severe political crisis.

events.title: Afghanistan: Shiite and other minorities living in fear
events.description: An attack on a Shiite place of worship in Herat killed least 11 people. While the Taliban have pledged protection, a sense of structural vulnerability is growing within the Shiite community.

events.title: Iran war: UN rights chief 'appalled' by arrests, executions
events.description: The UN rights office reports ongoing executions, mass arrests and a sweeping crackdown in Iran during the war, with thousands detained. Meanwhile, the country's currency has hit a record low. DW has the latest.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: UN rights chief 'appalled' by arrests, executions

events.title: Tuareg rebels urge Russian forces to leave Mali
events.description: Russian forces must withdraw from "all of Mali" and the ruling junta will "fall sooner or later", a spokesman for Tuareg rebels told AFP, after Islamist insurgents and separatists launched attacks destabilising the west African country. FRANCE 24's Juliette Brown reports.

events.title: REPLAY: US lawmakers grill Pentagon chief Hegseth over Iran war
events.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is facing questioning from lawmakers for the first time since US President Donald Trump launched the war against Iran, which Democrats have contested as a costly conflict of choice waged without congressional approval. Watch our replay.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 REPLAY: US lawmakers grill Pentagon chief Hegseth over Iran war

events.title: Man, 89, arrested after shootings leave several wounded in Athens
events.description: An 89-year-old man has been arrested after shootings in Athens left several people wounded.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Man, 89, arrested after shootings leave several wounded in Athens

events.title: UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict
events.description: The UN’s top human rights official warned on Wednesday that Iranians’ rights are being eroded in “harsh and brutal ways,” citing a surge in executions, mass arrests and alleged abuses amid a widening crackdown on dissent during the ongoing conflict.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UN rights chief warns of escalating crackdown in Iran amid conflict

events.title: Iran eyes revenge for Soleimani as WHCA Dinner shooting exposes security ‘vulnerability,’ expert warns
events.description: A former Pentagon intelligence officer claims the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner shooting exposed serious security vulnerabilities around Trump.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran eyes revenge for Soleimani as WHCA Dinner shooting exposes security ‘vulnerability,’ expert warns

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns
events.description: Kremlin-controlled paramilitaries also alleged it inflicted ‘irreplaceable losses’ on insurgents avoiding civilian casualties Russia’s defence ministry has claimed its Africa Corps – the successor to the former Wagner mercenary group – prevented a coup in Mali over the weekend, avoiding mass civilian casualties and inflicting “irreplaceable losses” on rebel insurgents. It said in a statement that its troops in the desert town of Kidal near the Algerian border had fought for more than 24 hours while completely surrounded and vastly outnumbered. It also alleged, without providing evidence, that the militants had been trained by European mercenary instructors, including Ukrainians. The casualty toll was not specified. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Russia claims its Africa Corps group prevented coup in Mali after rebels seize towns

events.title: Will UAE's departure from OPEC affect the oil cartel's future influence?
events.description: What are the impacts of the UAE's decision to pull out of the OPEC oil cartel? NPR's A Martinez speaks with Helima Croft, global head of commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets.

events.title: Firetrucks swarm Cologne Cathedral after smell of smoke
events.description: A large number of firetrucks were mobilized to the Cologne Cathedral after a churchwarden reported the smell of smoke.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Firetrucks swarm Cologne Cathedral after smell of smoke

events.title: Abuse at French after-school programmes: Parents sound the alarm
events.description: In Paris, parents are now dropping their children off at school in a climate of anxiety. For several months, reports of physical and sexual abuse involving nursery school children have been mounting. These cases are said to have taken place during after-school care and lunch breaks, when the children are supervised by non-teaching staff. In 2026 alone, 78 youth workers were suspended in Paris, 31 of them on suspicion of sexual abuse. Warning: viewers may find this report disturbing.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Abuse at French after-school programmes: Parents sound the alarm

events.title: Russia to hold Victory Day parade without weaponry display
events.description: The scaled back parade comes amid increasing Ukrainian attacks deep into mainland Russia, including the capital Moscow. The last time a Victory Day parade was held without weaponry display was in 2007.

events.title: France's public holidays: The art of the long weekend
events.description: France has a reputation, among some, of being a vacation nation. Is this warranted? In this edition of Entre Nous, we dive into France's public holidays during the month of May. We discuss the country's infamous long weekends or "ponts", an ongoing political fight over the May 1 bank holiday and what researchers say is the ideal number of "jours fériés" or public holidays.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France's public holidays: The art of the long weekend

events.title: Fresh oil shock as UAE exits Saudi-controlled cartels
events.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) has said it is to leave the OPEC and OPEC+ cartels of oil-producing nations, potentially threatening greater turbulence for global energy markets ahead.

events.title: Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 women teachers and health workers
events.description: Restrictions on girls’ education and women’s employment in Afghanistan could leave the country with a deficit of over 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030, the UN Children’s Fund (UNICEF) warned on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 women teachers and health workers

events.title: Feds hunt mystery drone thieves who swiped 15 sprayers that Army warned could be weaponized
events.description: Federal agents recovered 15 stolen industrial spray drones in New Jersey that a former FBI agent warned could pose a serious public safety danger.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Feds hunt mystery drone thieves who swiped 15 sprayers that Army warned could be weaponized

events.title: Iran turmoil erupts: Ultra-hardliner who mocked Trump poised to take over nuclear talks
events.description: Reports say veteran hardliner Saeed Jalili may replace Ghalibaf on Iran's negotiating team, deepening fractures within the regime's political leadership.

events.title: Comey surrenders over charge of threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
events.description: Prosecutors say a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director was a call for violence against Trump.

events.title: Comey charged with threatening Trump's life in Instagram post
events.description: The new case stems from a 2025 seashell photo posted by the former FBI director that the justice department says calls for violence against Trump.

events.title: Men accused of being approached by Russian contact to attack Starmer-linked assets
events.description: A British court has heard that three men from Ukraine and Romania were allegedly offered payment by a Russian-speaking contact to set fires linked to Prime Minister Keir Starmer

events.title: 2 suspects indicted in assault of TPUSA reporter Savanah Hernandez during Minneapolis ICE protest: sources
events.description: Two people face federal indictments for allegedly assaulting Turning Point USA reporter Savanah Hernandez at a Minneapolis ICE protest in April.

events.title: Man accused of repeatedly trespassing at CIA headquarters in Virginia
events.description: A Texas man was arrested after repeatedly trying to breach CIA headquarters in Langley, Virginia, over four days despite multiple warnings to stay away.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Man accused of repeatedly trespassing at CIA headquarters in Virginia

events.title: Florida man allegedly shot woman in neck, dragged her back inside before hiding in backyard pool
events.description: Florida man Robert Anthony Morgan allegedly shot a woman in the neck and threatened her mother after the shooting, Wildwood police say.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Florida man allegedly shot woman in neck, dragged her back inside before hiding in backyard pool

events.title: Illegal immigrant accused of killing newlywed couple in Oregon crash arrested by ICE after jail release
events.description: Illegal immigrant Rajinder Kumar was arrested by ICE after being released from jail by Oregon officials, DHS says. He is accused of causing a crash that killed a newlywed couple.

events.title: US Fed holds rates steady in Powell’s final meeting as Fed chair
events.description: Central bank keeps interest rates steady at 3.5-3.75 percent amid inflation concerns and global economic strain.

events.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
events.description: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

events.title: Mali: France urges citizens to leave amid Tuareg advance
events.description: With Tuareg-led rebels saying the ruling junta in Mali will fall "sooner or later" and demanding the withdrawal of Russian troops, the French Foreign Ministry has called on French nationals to leave the country.

events.title: Hot weather and hungry datacentres lift Australia’s energy demand to record highs but batteries quell prices
events.description: Rise in electricity demand in first quarter of 2026 was moderated by record output from rooftop solar Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast More datacentres and warmer conditions helped push electricity demand to record highs in the first three months of the year, according to Australia’s Energy Market Operator, while growth in batteries kept average wholesale prices down. Electricity demand – from households, business and industry – reached record levels of 25GW in Q1 2026, an increase of 1.2% compared with the same quarter last year. Across the grid, this growth was offset by record output from rooftop solar. Continue reading...

events.title: ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards
events.description: Delegates at event in Cape Verde highlight opportunities from tech while stressing AI is no replacement for talent Last July, the Nigerian singer-songwriter Fave found herself caught up in a viral moment: an unauthorised version of a track by her featuring an AI choir had been released, quickly becoming an internet sensation. To get ahead of the situation, she recorded her own remix that integrated the AI-assisted song and added it to her discography. “In my view, [that] was smart and very business aware,” Oyinkansola Fawehinmi, a Lagos-based entertainment lawyer, observed a few months later . “She essentially reclaimed the ‘AI version’ and released it as her own official expression.” Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘It will never cover what’s authentic’: African music industry weighs up AI risks and rewards

events.title: Indian billionaire’s son offers to house Pablo Escobar’s hippos at his private zoo
events.description: Anant Ambani revives offer to transport 80 animals, all descendants of Colombian drug kingpin’s pets, to India It remains one of the strangest conundrums in modern zoological history – what to do with the descendants of Pablo Escobar’s hippos? The animals – herbivores native to sub-Saharan Africa – were originally imported into Colombia by the drug kingpin for his own entertainment. But the beasts and their offspring were left to roam free after his death in 1993. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia’s use of methamphetamine has doubled in a decade, wastewater monitoring reveals
events.description: Consumption is at a record high along with that of cocaine, Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission figures show Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Methamphetamine use in Australia has almost doubled in the past decade and stimulants are being taken at record highs , new wastewater monitoring reveals. On Wednesday evening the Australian Criminal Intelligence Commission (Acic) released its latest annual report after testing wastewater samples from 64 treatment plants across the country between August 2024 and 2025. events.title: Pakistan in the middle: What benefits and risks from US-Iran mediator?
events.description: There are those who want the US and Iran to settle and those who want regime change. Among the latter, there’s Israel of course and then there’s the United Arab Emirates who’ve signaled OPEC’s cautious response as a reason for quitting the cartel. The end of OPEC? How will its biggest producer Saudi Arabia respond? The Emirates also seem to have it in for Saudi ally Pakistan, suddenly demanding the immediate reimbursement of a 3-and-a-half billion dollar loan from the cash-strapped facilitators of US-Iran mediation efforts.

Japan Airlines will introduce humanoid robots on a trial basis from the beginning of May, with a view to deploying them permanently as a solution to the country’s chronic labour shortage . events.title: Armenia's FM hails peace, embraces future: EU, elections, trade, 'huge' infrastructure projects

He asserts that “we now have peace between Armenia and Azerbaijan,” framing it not as a symbolic declaration but as an emerging reality, further underscored by the intention to “institutionalise this peace.”

events.title: Libya ‘corruption pact’: Sarkozy returns to court as his former right-hand man turns against him

Guéant’s statements – and Sarkozy’s denials – are the latest chapter in a public falling-out as Sarkozy appeals his conviction for illegally accepting Libyan funds for his 2007 presidential campaign.

events.title: US supreme court’s conservative judges appear amenable to stripping TPS for Haitians and Syrians

During the arguments, justices in the conservative-leaning majority appeared sympathetic to the Trump administration’s attempts to strip humanitarian protections for the Syrians and Haitians in this case. events.title: Kim praises North Korean soldiers who blew themselves up to evade Ukraine capture

events.description: Basel Zaraa was a Palestinian refugee. When his 5-year-old daughter started asking him questions, he told her this story.

events.title: How the U.K. is reacting to King Charles' visit to the U.S.
events.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Lord Peter Ricketts, a member of the House of Lords and a former British diplomat, about King Charles' visit to the U.S. during a tumultuous time.

events.title: Germany: Merz hails 'historic' health care reform
events.description: The Cabinet has greenlit a health care draft law designed to lower insurance rates. The bill heads to parliament next, but doctors' associations and patient protection advocates say it amounts to austerity measures.

events.title: Hungary inspires Israeli opposition bid to defeat Netanyahu
events.description: Taking a cue from the ousting of Hungary's long-time leader, Viktor Orban, two Israeli opposition figures have joined forces to try to unseat Prime Minister Netanyahu.

events.title: 'Rose' and the piece of fabric that embodies gender politics
events.description: As the film starring Sandra Hüller is released in Germany, DW talked to director Markus Schleinzer about why women passing as men inspired his historical drama, and why it's a current topic.

events.title: Thailand ex-PM Thaksin Shinawatra is granted early parole
events.description: The influential former prime minister of Thailand, Thaksin Shinawatra, is set to be released from prison in May. He was serving a one-year prison term for corruption that began in September.

events.title: Why UAE's OPEC exit is a blow to Saudi Arabia
events.description: The United Arab Emirates is leaving OPEC to pump more oil on its own terms. The break strips Saudi Arabia of a key partner and adds to growing uncertainty over the cartel's future.

events.title: South Korea: Yoon's obstruction sentence hiked to 7 years
events.description: Yoon has also been handed a life sentence for leading an insurrection in a separate trial. The prison sentences follow his failed attempt to impose martial law in 2024.

events.title: Hungary's Magyar meets EU's von der Leyen in high-stakes Brussels visit
events.description: Even before Peter Magyar takes office as Hungary's prime minister, he is looking to unlock €10 billion in EU funding ahead of an August deadline. Talks with Ursula von der Leyen come as part of a post-Orban EU reset.

events.title: How Iranian diplomats turned embassy accounts into viral meme machines
events.description: Memes, humour, and trolling of American politicians: This is how a handful of Iranian embassies have become superstars on “X”, attracting millions of views. But how did the old-fashioned and largely ignored accounts of Iran’s diplomats in South Africa, Thailand, and the United Kingdom suddenly turn into Gen Z-style viral feeds, with millions of views?

events.title: Genius & Gadgets: Discovering Paris’s annual invention competition
events.description: France has a long, slightly eccentric tradition of invention…did you know some of the revolutionary items you use everyday were first introduced to the public at Paris’s annual invention competition?

events.title: US forces board ship suspected of heading to Iran port
events.description: US Marines on Tuesday boarded a commercial ship in the Arabian Sea that was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade of Iranian ports, the US military said. The M/V Blue Star III was released by US forces "after conducting a search and confirming the ship's voyage would not include an Iranian port call," Central Command, which is responsible for American forces in the Middle East, said in a post on X.

events.title: Cartel leader tipped to succeed 'El Mencho' captured
events.description: Mexico's army has captured a cartel leader tipped to succeed notorious drug lord "El Mencho".

events.title: True identity of woman's body revealed more than 20 years after being found by roadside
events.description: The true identity of a woman whose body was discovered in a rainwater collector has been revealed after more than 20 years.

events.title: Thailand ex-PM Thaksin is set for prison release next month after getting parole
events.description: Former Thai Prime Minister Thaksin Shinawatra is set to be released from a Bangkok prison next month after a Justice Ministry panel decision to grant him parole

events.title: He once struck a peace deal with Israel and says Lebanon's leaders should try again now
events.description: Former Lebanese President Amin Gemayel believes the time might be right to pursue peace talks with Israel

events.title: UK expels Russian diplomat in retaliation for Moscow’s recent expulsion of a British official
events.description: The U.K. has expelled a Russian diplomat in retaliation for Moscow’s recent expulsion of a British official and the smear campaign that followed

events.title: Cubans back 'My signature for the Homeland' campaign as tensions with US intensify
events.description: Cubans have been endorsing the socialist government’s latest move to defy the U.S. as tensions between the two countries escalate

events.title: A son of late Zimbabwe leader Mugabe is ordered deported from South Africa
events.description: A magistrate has ordered the deportation of Bellarmine Mugabe, son of the late Zimbabwean President Robert Mugabe, from South Africa

events.title: Philippine congressional committee rules there's evidence to impeach vice president
events.description: A Philippine congressional committee has found probable cause to impeach Vice President Sara Duterte

events.title: Lebanon detains ex-Palestinian envoy at Beirut airport on corruption charges
events.description: Lebanese authorities have detained the former Palestinian ambassador to Lebanon on corruption charges

events.title: Former South Korean president gets 7 years for charges including resisting arrest
events.description: A South Korean appeals court on Wednesday sentenced ousted President Yoon Suk Yeol to seven years in prison for resisting arrest and bypassing a legitimate Cabinet meeting before his brief imposition of martial law in December 2024

events.title: High school sweethearts' marriage ends in gruesome murder-suicide less than 2 years after wedding: police
events.description: Pennsylvania authorities say high school sweethearts Ryan Hosso and Madeline Spatafore died in a murder-suicide less than two years after their 2024 wedding.
events.ticker_label: 💥 High school sweethearts' marriage ends in gruesome murder-suicide less than 2 years after wedding: police

events.title: Scott Peterson's defense loses bid to introduce witness testimony in long-running fight to overturn conviction
events.description: Judge rejects convicted killer Scott Peterson's claims linking wife Laci's disappearance to a nearby burglary. His defense team plans to appeal the ruling.

events.title: DNA cracks 35-year cold case mystery as accused killer’s new name melts under cops’ heat
events.description: James Lawhead Jr. was arrested in Arizona after DNA testing and facial recognition linked him to the 1991 kidnapping and murder of Cindy Wanner, police said.

events.title: Former FBI director James Comey's bombshell indictment and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Former FBI director James Comey's bombshell indictment and more top headlines

events.title: Alleged Trump assassination plot: Analysis shows 1 in 5 left-wing posts cry hoax, admin shreds ‘moron’ claims
events.description: Online conspiracy theories spiked after a gunman allegedly attempted to assassinate President Trump at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner Saturday.
events.ticker_label: 🚨 Alleged Trump assassination plot: Analysis shows 1 in 5 left-wing posts cry hoax, admin shreds ‘moron’ claims

events.title: Texas elementary school teacher accused of sexually assaulting child as police search for more victims
events.description: A Texas elementary school teacher is accused of continuous sexual assault of a child. Police say there may be additional victims and are asking for tips.

events.title: Record antisemitic incidents in Canada fuel criticism of Carney government response
events.description: Canada faces record antisemitic incidents in 2025 as critics say Prime Minister Mark Carney's government is failing to adequately address anti-Jewish hate.

events.title: Could Narges Mohammadi unite Iran’s opposition? Husband says imprisoned Nobel laureate still fighting
events.description: Narges Mohammadi's husband says the jailed Nobel Peace Prize laureate was severely beaten during arrest but remains politically unbroken in prison.
